label: Recurly
description: Connect to Recurly to access subscription billing data including plans, accounts, invoices, and transactions.

form.title: Connect Recurly
form.subtitle: Paste your Recurly private API key to give Anton access to your subscription billing data.

methods.label: Private API Key
methods.description: Paste your Recurly private API key. Found under Integrations → API Credentials in your Recurly dashboard. Quickest way to connect — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Recurly Private API Key

Recurly issues a private API key per site that grants full API access. Treat it like a password.

### Steps

1. Log in to your [Recurly dashboard](https://app.recurly.com).
2. In the left sidebar, navigate to **Integrations → API Credentials**.
3. Under **Private API Key**, click **Show** (or **Regenerate** if you want a fresh key).
4. Copy the full key — it starts with a prefix like `recurly-`.
5. Paste it into the **Private API Key** field below.
6. Optionally enter your **Recurly Site Subdomain** (e.g. `mycompany`) if you want Anton to target a specific site. You can find this in **Admin → Site Settings** or in your dashboard URL (`mycompany.recurly.com`).

### Notes

- The private API key grants read and write access to your Recurly site. Store it securely and rotate it in the dashboard if it is ever exposed.
- If your account has multiple sites, make sure you copy the key from the correct site's API Credentials page.
- Recurly also issues a public key for client-side tokenization — that is not the key needed here.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Private API Key
fields.description: Found in your Recurly dashboard under Integrations → API Credentials. Starts with 'recurly-'.

fields.name: subdomain
fields.label: Site Subdomain
fields.description: Your Recurly site subdomain (e.g. 'mycompany' from mycompany.recurly.com). Required if your account manages multiple sites.
